frameworks.name: running-average evidence integration
frameworks.one_line: TEa encodes a running mean of task-relevant evidence as decision variable

frameworks.name: bounded accumulation with ramping
frameworks.one_line: canonical frontoparietal ramp signature of integration to bound

frameworks.name: subunit model of decision dynamics
frameworks.one_line: combines momentary evidence with running-average DV to fit TEa responses

frameworks.name: ventral visual pathway for object decisions
frameworks.one_line: repositions inferotemporal cortex as decision-aware, not purely sensory

core_question: Does anterior inferotemporal cortex (TEa) implement evidence integration during object-based decisions, and in what format?
key_assumptions: stochastic face categorization isolates evidence integration for object features
simultaneous LIP recording allows inheritance-from-feedback testing
running-mean code is a viable alternative to bounded ramp accumulation
manipulations: momentary feature fluctuations across stochastic face stimuli
pre- vs post-commitment epochs to probe sensitivity collapse
simultaneous TEa and LIP recordings during the same trials
key_findings: TEa neurons track momentary feature fluctuations and an accumulated DV concurrently
DV is encoded as running average of task-relevant evidence, without ramps
category SNR increases via flexible amplification of task-relevant features
sensitivity to new evidence sharply collapses after commitment
interpretations: TEa implements a previously unrecognized non-ramp format for evidence integration
TEa acts as a decision-aware hub within the ventral visual pathway, not inherited from LIP
